data.title: Global nuclear energy leaders at WNA Symposium warn of uranium shortage by 2030; demand rising sharply amid plans to triple capacity by 2050.
data.description: The World Nuclear Association hosted its 50th Symposium in London, where global nuclear energy leaders discussed the increasing electricity consumption by data centers. They predict that next year, data centers will consume as much electricity as Japan. Experts believe that tripling nuclear energy capacity by 2050 is necessary to meet demands, but a uranium shortage threatens this goal. The demand for fuel is rising sharply while supplies from existing mines are expected to halve between 2030 and 2040. The WNA is urging miners to speed up exploration and investment to prevent a potential crisis that could hinder the role of nuclear energy in meeting global energy needs. Additionally, the article mentions the rally of the VanEck Uranium ETF by 44% due to Trump's nuclear energy push and the pursuit of AI-powered energy solutions by companies like Microsoft, Google, and Nvidia.

data.title: Volunteers impacted by FSH Muscular Dystrophy rally for fundraising events across US and Canada in Sept-Oct 2025.
data.description: In September and October of 2025, the Walk & Roll to Cure FSHD will convene communities in the U.S. and Canada with the goal of raising funds and awareness for FSHD. The events bring together families, friends, and neighbors in Randolph, Mass. to support this cause.

data.title: Anika granted 2,500 stock options at $9.15 per share under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4) for new employee.
data.description: On September 2, 2025, Anika Therapeutics, Inc. granted 2,500 non-statutory stock options for common stock at an exercise price of $9.15 per share to a newly hired non-executive employee. This grant, equaling the closing price of common stock on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on the grant date, is part of the Anika Therapeutics, Inc. 2021 Inducement Plan. The approval for this grant came from the compensation committee of the board of directors and aligns with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4) as it serves as a material inducement for the employee's acceptance of employment with Anika.

data.title: Deportations rise to match slow hiring amid decreasing labor demand, maintaining a 'curious balance'.
data.description: The arrival of a substantial number of immigrants alleviated worker shortages, but now their removal is concealing the country's diminishing need for labor.
